summary: Build-configuration PR that changed the project away from a `--non-matching` switch toward an explicit `--must-match` mode, then marked many formerly `Matching` objects as `NonMatching` in `configure.py` so non-matching C can exist without being linked as matching output. It also added `BUGFIX` to compile flags, adjusted several source guards so bug-fixed/non-matching builds can compile different code from strict matching builds, gave runtime libraries their own cflags path, removed `-gccinc` from runtime cflags, and changed GALE01 section alignment from 8 to 32.
what_changed: Renamed the configure option from `--non-matching` to `--must-match` and changed the behavior from `if not args.non_matching: -DMUST_MATCH` to `if args.must_match: -DMUST_MATCH`.

Updated the option help text to say `define MUST_MATCH for make compatibility`, clarifying that `MUST_MATCH` is now an explicit compatibility/matching mode rather than the default opposite of non-matching.
Extended `Lib(...)` to accept a `cflags` argument defaulting to `cflags_base`, and changed `RuntimeLib(...)` to pass `cflags=cflags_runtime`.
Removed `-gccinc` from the runtime cflags list shown in the diff excerpt.

Changed many `Object(Matching, ...)` entries in `configure.py` to `Object(NonMatching, ...)`, especially across `melee/lb`, `melee/pl/player.c`, many `melee/ft` files, common fighter-state files, and character special files.

Changed all top-level GALE01 section alignments in `config/GALE01/splits.txt` from `align:8` to `align:32` for `.init`, `extab`, `extabindex`, `.text`, `.ctors`, `.dtors`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2`.

Changed several source guards from plain `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` to `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) || !defined(BUGFIX)` for unused/force-active data and assert behavior in `OSAlloc.c`, `cobj.c`, `debug.h`, and `mobj.c`.
Changed `lb_00B0.c` from `#ifndef MUST_MATCH` to `#ifdef BUGFIX` around a `return NULL;` path.
Changed `tev.c` so the inline asm `HSD_SetupChannel` block is guarded by `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` instead of `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO`.
Replaced direct `__assert(__FILE__, line, "0")` calls in several `tev.c` default cases with `HSD_ASSERT(line, 0)` and changed some allocator-size literals from hexadecimal to decimal.
smart_moves: Made strict matching opt-in with `--must-match`, which reduces ambiguity around whether the build is intended to reproduce the original binary or compile convenient non-matching/development code.

Used existing `Object(Matching, ...)` versus `Object(NonMatching, ...)` metadata as the central place to unlink files from the matching build, instead of scattering per-file build exceptions elsewhere.
Introduced a separate cflags parameter for libraries, letting `RuntimeLib` use `cflags_runtime` without forcing all libraries through the same flag set.

Separated matching-only artifacts from bug-fixed development builds with `MUST_MATCH` and `BUGFIX` guards, preserving old binary-sensitive unused data/assert paths when necessary while permitting cleaner behavior in normal development builds.
Kept `compile_flags.txt` in sync by adding `-DBUGFIX`, so editor tooling and compile-database consumers see the same macro assumptions as the intended non-matching build.
decomp_lessons: Do not mark a translation unit as `Matching` in `configure.py` just because it compiles; only use `Matching` when it should participate in strict linked matching output.
Prefer a positive `--must-match` switch over a negative/non-default `--non-matching` switch when the build system needs to distinguish strict reproduction from working decompiled code.

When non-matching code intentionally fixes undefined behavior or missing returns, gate those fixes with a dedicated macro such as `BUGFIX` rather than overloading `MUST_MATCH` for every development-build difference.
Runtime libraries may need cflags that differ from game libraries; make the library constructor accept explicit cflags rather than relying on one global list.
Inline asm fallbacks or matching-only asm blocks should be guarded narrowly, as in `defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)`, so WIP/non-matching C paths can still build.

Section alignment settings in splits files are part of reproducibility; this PR changed GALE01 section alignment to 32, so future split edits should preserve the intended alignment unless there is matching evidence to change it.
naming_conventions: `MUST_MATCH` is treated as the macro for strict/make-compatible matching behavior.
`BUGFIX` is treated as the macro for development/non-matching builds that allow corrected behavior such as returning `NULL` or omitting original unused artifacts.
`Object(Matching, "path.c")` and `Object(NonMatching, "path.c")` are the build-system convention for whether an object is linked into matching output.
`HSD_ASSERT(line, cond)` is preferred over open-coded `__assert(__FILE__, line, "0")` in the touched `sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c` code.
The configure option name `--must-match` communicates positive intent more clearly than the removed `--non-matching` option.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved `HSD_SetupChannel` inline asm in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/tev.c` behind `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)`, keeping asm for strict matching while allowing WIP/non-matching alternatives.

Kept force-active unused strings/floats in files such as `OSAlloc.c`, `cobj.c`, and `mobj.c` when `MUST_MATCH` is set or when `BUGFIX` is not defined, preserving binary-sensitive artifacts outside bug-fixed builds.
Changed many object statuses from `Matching` to `NonMatching` rather than editing their source to force a match, indicating the intended tactic was unlinking imperfect C from the matching target.
Adjusted GALE01 section alignment from 8 to 32, a linker/split-level matching tactic rather than a source-level code change.
